reasoning: On June 22, 2025, the U.S. and Israel engaged in major military airstrikes against three Iranian nuclear sites. However, the resolution criteria clearly stipulate that the U.S. must commence a "military offensive intended to establish control over any portion of Iran." Top officials, specifically mentioned in news reports (like President Trump and VP JD Vance), explicitly ruled out any "boots on the ground" or ground invasion of Iran. Furthermore, military analysts and intelligence updates up through October 26, 2025, show no evidence of the massive troop build-ups or logistical staging that would be required for a ground invasion of a large, heavily fortified nation like Iran within the remaining two months of the year. The Polymarket price stood at a very low 3% in mid-October, heavily aligning with the assessment that an invasion to capture territory is highly improbable. I estimate a 2% chance to account for extremely unlikely edge cases (like seizing a tiny island in a rapid escalation).
